childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

attributes.title: Meta mode — edit or ask about this story / kitsoki

node.textContent: Every run begins here, in the story library. We'll demonstrate the async inbox on a tiny story whose only job is to launch background work — exactly the shape that produces a runtime notification.
This story dispatches a long-running task in the background. When the task finishes, the runtime posts a notification — and that is what the global inbox surfaces, no matter which room you're in.
Click New session to create a fresh, independently-traced run of the story and drop into its interactive chat view.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.

node.textContent:
Async Inbox Demo
Ready to launch a background task.
This room launches a long-running task in the background. When it finishes, a
notification lands in your global inbox — watch the badge.

Status:  idle
Actions

• start  kick off the background task
• check
Click start to kick off the background host call. It runs asynchronously — you keep control of the session while it works, and the inbox will tell you when it's done.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.

attributes.title: Call did not complete or its completion was not recorded

node.textContent:
Async Inbox Demo
Background task running.
The task is running in the background. You can keep working — the inbox will
notify you when it completes.

Job:     01KW3AJJ80F6SN40KP8R9X2MV7
Status:  running
Actions

• check

For a success or action-required notification the inbox raises a transient toast the instant the background job completes. It auto-dismisses after a few seconds — or you can click it to jump straight to the room the work came from.
The global inbox badge lights up

Even after the toast fades the count persists on the global inbox badge — fixed in the chrome, visible on every route. When the background job reached a terminal state the runtime posted a notification, the server fanned it out over the global notifications SSE stream, and this badge incremented. The same badge also counts active work such as queued or failed subagents, while attention color is reserved for work that needs intervention.

attributes.title: Story definition is up-to-date with the file on disk
Refresh assigned GitHub issues and requested PR reviews

node.textContent: Opening the badge reveals the full inbox panel: prioritized active work first, then notification history. Notification-backed work, including matching job rows, offers a jump action backed by the same teleport target as the toast; failed subagent rows open focused chat context.

attributes.title: Story definition is up-to-date with the file on disk

node.textContent: Click 'jump →' on the notification. The inbox teleports the session back to the exact room the background job was launched from — the notification carries that origin state, so a one-click jump lands you where the work happened.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.

A background turn posted a runtime notification, the global badge reflected it alongside the active-work queue, a toast surfaced it, and one click teleported the session back to its originating room — all deterministic, no LLM. Hit '?' anytime to replay this tour.
